title: Mountainous Monsoon Norwegian Pennarigata Pasta
description: A floral, cheese-pull monsoon Norwegian dish featuring penne rigate, served ice-bath-shocked. Perfect for an ayurvedic sattvic-friendly breakfast or snack.
instructions: Cook the penne rigate in boiling salted water until al dente.
Drain the penne rigate and set it aside to cool.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the all-purpose flour and whisk to form a smooth paste.
Slowly pour in the milk and heavy cream, whisking continuously.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it thickens.
Stir in the grated Gruy3re until melted and smooth.
Add the cooked penne rigate, edamame, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Transfer the penne rigate mixture to an ice bath and shock for 5 minutes.
Drain the penne rigate mixture and stir in the truffle oil, if using.
Transfer the penne rigate mixture to a serving dish and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley and chopped fresh dill.
Garnish with additional chopped fresh parsley and dill, if desired.
